@fileExplorer.archiveEnterMenu.ariaLabel.description: Accessible name for the popup menu that appears when the user presses Enter on an archive or app bundle set to "Ask". The menu offers browse, open, or configure.

@fileExplorer.archiveEnterMenu.browse.description: Menu item: step inside the archive or bundle and list its contents like a normal folder.

@fileExplorer.archiveEnterMenu.open.description: Menu item: hand the archive or bundle to its default app instead of browsing it (a .zip opens in the OS archive tool; a .app launches).

@fileExplorer.archiveEnterMenu.configure.description: Menu item: open Settings to change what pressing Enter does for this format. The trailing ellipsis (…) signals that a settings window opens; keep it.

@fileExplorer.breadcrumb.navigateTooltip.description: Tooltip on a clickable path segment in a pane's breadcrumb. Clicking the segment opens that ancestor folder.

placeholders.path: the friendly path of the ancestor folder the click navigates to, e.g. ~/projects

fileExplorer.doubleClickHint.title: What just happened?

@fileExplorer.doubleClickHint.title.description: Title of the one-time notification shown the first time a double-click on a pane''s empty background navigates up to the parent folder.

fileExplorer.doubleClickHint.body: You double-clicked the pane background. This navigates to the parent folder.

@fileExplorer.doubleClickHint.body.description: Body of the one-time double-click-to-parent notification, explaining what the gesture did. "pane" = one of the two file-list columns.

@fileExplorer.doubleClickHint.dontLikeIt.description: Short prompt next to the two buttons in the double-click-to-parent notification, inviting the user to turn the behavior off.

@fileExplorer.doubleClickHint.neverAgain.description: Secondary button in the double-click-to-parent notification. Turns the double-click-to-parent behavior off.

@fileExplorer.doubleClickHint.iLikeIt.description: Primary button in the double-click-to-parent notification. Keeps the behavior on and dismisses the notification.

@fileExplorer.pane.filePaneAriaLabel.description: Accessibility label for one of the two file-list panes. The app always has a left and a right pane; this names which one.

placeholders.side: which pane this is: the value is either left or right

@fileExplorer.pane.diskUsageAriaLabel.description: Accessibility label for the small disk-usage bar shown under a pane, indicating how full the current volume is.

@fileExplorer.pane.resizePanesAriaLabel.description: Accessibility label for the draggable divider between the two file panes. Dragging it changes how the width is split.

@fileExplorer.pane.fileExplorerAriaLabel.description: Accessibility label for the whole two-pane file explorer region.

@fileExplorer.pane.disconnectFailedToast.description: Error toast when disconnecting from a network server did not work. {message} is the underlying reason text.

placeholders.message: the underlying failure reason, already worded for the user

@fileExplorer.pane.connectedDirectlyToas.description: Success toast after upgrading a network share to a faster direct connection.

@fileExplorer.pane.directConnectionFaile.description: Error toast when upgrading a network share to a faster direct connection did not work. {message} is the underlying reason text.

placeholders.message: the underlying failure reason, already worded for the user

fileExplorer.pane.trashUnconfirmedToast: Couldn''t confirm the file was moved to Trash. The volume may be slow, but the file may still have been moved. Please check your Trash to be sure.

@fileExplorer.pane.trashUnconfirmedToast.description: Persistent warning toast shown when moving a file to the Trash (as part of a rename-overwrite) timed out, so Cmdr can''t confirm it landed. Reassures the user it may have worked and asks them to check. "Trash" is the macOS Trash; keep the name.

@fileExplorer.pane.ejectFailedToast.description: Error toast when ejecting a drive or volume did not work. {volumeName} is the drive''s display name; {message} is the underlying reason text.

placeholders.volumeName: the display name of the drive or volume being ejected
placeholders.message: the underlying failure reason, already worded for the user

@fileExplorer.errorPane.tryAgain.description: Button on the error screen shown in place of a file list when a folder could not be opened. Retries opening it.

@fileExplorer.errorPane.openSystemSettin.description: Button on the folder error screen that opens the macOS System Settings app (so the user can grant access). {systemSettings} is the OS name for that app.

placeholders.systemSettings: the localized OS name of the System Settings app (do not translate; comes from the OS)

@fileExplorer.errorPane.technicalDetails.description: Label for a collapsible disclosure on the folder error screen that reveals raw diagnostic info.

@fileExplorer.errorPane.aMomentAgo.description: Relative time shown in the folder error screen''s diagnostic details, for something that happened within the last few seconds.

@fileExplorer.errorPane.secondsAgo.description: Relative time in the folder error diagnostics: how many seconds ago. Shown compactly, e.g. 12s ago.

@fileExplorer.errorPane.minutesAgo.description: Relative time in the folder error diagnostics: how many minutes ago. Shown compactly, e.g. 5m ago.

@fileExplorer.errorPane.hoursAgo.description: Relative time in the folder error diagnostics: how many hours ago. Shown compactly, e.g. 3h ago.

@fileExplorer.errorPane.retryInfo.description: Diagnostic line on the folder error screen summarizing repeated failures. The dot (·) is a separator. {firstAgo} is a relative time like "5m ago".

placeholders.count: how many times opening this folder has been retried
placeholders.firstAgo: relative time of the first attempt, e.g. 5m ago

@fileExplorer.errorPane.retryInfoWithLas.description: Same as the retry-info diagnostic line, but also including the most recent attempt''s relative time. The dots (·) are separators.

placeholders.count: how many times opening this folder has been retried
placeholders.firstAgo: relative time of the first attempt, e.g. 5m ago
placeholders.lastAgo: relative time of the most recent attempt, e.g. 1m ago

@fileExplorer.functionKeyBar.toolbarAria.description: Accessibility label for the bottom toolbar of function-key buttons (the F1-F8 style row of file actions).

@fileExplorer.functionKeyBar.actionWithS.description: Accessibility label for a function-key button, naming the action and the keyboard shortcut that triggers it, e.g. "Copy (F5)". The shortcut updates live if the user rebinds the key.

placeholders.action: the full action name, e.g. Copy, Rename, Delete permanently
placeholders.shortcut: the keyboard shortcut glyphs, e.g. F5 or ⌘C (do not translate; these are key names)

@fileExplorer.functionKeyBar.noShiftActi.description: Accessibility label for a disabled function-key slot that has no action while Shift is held. {fnKey} is the key name like F2.

@fileExplorer.functionKeyBar.newFileLabe.description: Short button label on the function-key bar for creating a new file. Space is tight; keep it short.

@fileExplorer.functionKeyBar.newFileActi.description: The full spoken action name (for screen readers) of the new-file function-key button. Longer form of the short button label.

@fileExplorer.functionKeyBar.renameLabel.description: Button label on the function-key bar for renaming the file under the cursor. Space is tight; keep it short.

@fileExplorer.functionKeyBar.renameActio.description: The full spoken action name (for screen readers) of the rename function-key button.

@fileExplorer.functionKeyBar.permanently.description: Short button label (shown while Shift is held) for permanently deleting the selected files, bypassing the trash. Space is tight; the full action is spoken separately.

@fileExplorer.functionKeyBar.deletePerma.description: The full spoken action name (for screen readers) of the permanent-delete function-key button (bypasses the trash).

@fileExplorer.functionKeyBar.viewLabel.description: Short button label on the function-key bar for viewing the file under the cursor in the built-in viewer. Space is tight; keep it short.

@fileExplorer.functionKeyBar.viewAction.description: The full spoken action name (for screen readers) of the view-file function-key button.

@fileExplorer.functionKeyBar.editLabel.description: Short button label on the function-key bar for editing the file under the cursor. Space is tight; keep it short.

@fileExplorer.functionKeyBar.editAction.description: The full spoken action name (for screen readers) of the edit-file function-key button.

@fileExplorer.functionKeyBar.copyLabel.description: Button label on the function-key bar for copying the selected files to the other pane. Space is tight; keep it short.

@fileExplorer.functionKeyBar.copyAction.description: The full spoken action name (for screen readers) of the copy function-key button.

@fileExplorer.functionKeyBar.moveLabel.description: Button label on the function-key bar for moving the selected files to the other pane. Space is tight; keep it short.

@fileExplorer.functionKeyBar.moveAction.description: The full spoken action name (for screen readers) of the move function-key button.

@fileExplorer.functionKeyBar.newFolderLa.description: Button label on the function-key bar for creating a new folder. Space is tight; keep it short.

@fileExplorer.functionKeyBar.newFolderAc.description: The full spoken action name (for screen readers) of the new-folder function-key button.

@fileExplorer.functionKeyBar.deleteLabel.description: Button label on the function-key bar for deleting the selected files (to the trash). Space is tight; keep it short.

@fileExplorer.typeToJump.prefix.description: Visible label before the typed characters in the type-to-jump indicator (the typed text follows in its own styled span). Keep the trailing space.

@fileExplorer.searchResults.unavailableB.description: Body of the empty-state message shown when a saved search-results view''s data was cleared, telling the user to run a new search.

@fileExplorer.network.connectDialog.exam.description: Help text under the connect-to-server field giving example addresses. The example values (mynas.local, the IP, smb://server/share) are literals; keep them as-is.

@fileExplorer.network.browser.colName.description: Column header in the network host list for the host''s display name. Tight column; keep it short.

@fileExplorer.network.browser.colIp.description: Column header in the network host list for the host''s IP address. Tight column; keep it short.

@fileExplorer.network.browser.colHostnam.description: Column header in the network host list for the host''s network hostname. Tight column; keep it short.

@fileExplorer.network.browser.colStatus.description: Column header in the network host list for the host''s connection status. Tight column; keep it short.

@fileExplorer.network.browser.fetching.description: Placeholder shown in the network host list''s IP or hostname cell while that value is still being resolved. Lowercase, matching the quiet inline style.

@fileExplorer.network.browser.unknown.description: Placeholder in the network host list''s Shares cell when the share count could not be determined. Shown in parentheses.

@fileExplorer.network.browser.searching.description: Status shown while the app is still discovering network hosts (mDNS browsing in progress).

@fileExplorer.network.browser.connectToS.description: Label of the always-present last row in the network host list that opens the manual connect-to-server dialog. The trailing ellipsis signals a dialog follows.

@fileExplorer.network.browser.noHostsTit.description: Empty-state title in the network browser when no SMB hosts were discovered on the network.

fileExplorer.network.browser.noHostsMess: Make sure you''re on a network with SMB-capable devices.

@fileExplorer.network.browser.noHostsMes.description: Empty-state body in the network browser advising the user to check their network. "SMB" is the file-sharing protocol name; do not translate it.

@fileExplorer.network.browser.refresh.description: Button in the empty network browser that re-scans the network for hosts.

fileExplorer.network.browser.refreshHint: Press <key></key> or click here to refresh

@fileExplorer.network.browser.refreshHin.description: Hint in the network status bar telling the user how to refresh the host list. The empty <key></key> marks where a keyboard-shortcut chip (showing ⌘R) is rendered inline; keep the tag in the sentence and reorder the surrounding words for natural grammar.

placeholders.key: marks where the keyboard-shortcut chip is inserted inline (the chip shows the ⌘R key combo); keep the tag, it carries no translatable text

fileExplorer.dirSize.noPermsTooltip: Cmdr lacks permission to read this folder

@fileExplorer.dirSize.noPermsTooltip.description: Hover tooltip on the no-permission size placeholder, explaining why the folder''s size is unavailable.

@fileExplorer.dirSize.noFiles.description: Phrase in a folder''s size tooltip for the file count when the folder contains zero files. Leads the count line, e.g. "No files, no folders". Capitalized as the start of the line.

@fileExplorer.dirSize.noFolders.description: Phrase in a folder''s size tooltip for the folder count when there are zero subfolders. Follows the file count, e.g. "3 files, no folders". Lowercase since it follows a comma.

@fileExplorer.dirSize.fileCount.description: Recursive file count in a folder''s size tooltip, e.g. "1,234 files". Combined with the folder count into a line like "1,234 files, 12 folders".

placeholders.countText: the number of files, already formatted for display
placeholders.count: the same number of files (drives the singular/plural form)

@fileExplorer.dirSize.folderCount.description: Recursive folder count in a folder''s size tooltip, e.g. "12 folders". Combined with the file count into a line like "1,234 files, 12 folders".

placeholders.countText: the number of folders, already formatted for display
placeholders.count: the same number of folders (drives the singular/plural form)

fileExplorer.dirSize.updatingIndexLine: Updating index, so size may change.

@fileExplorer.dirSize.updatingIndexLine.description: Last line of a folder''s size tooltip while indexing, warning the figure may change.

@fileExplorer.dirSize.dirPlaceholder.description: Placeholder shown in the Size column for a folder whose recursive size is not computed yet. Rendered literally as <dir> (with the angle brackets). The leading apostrophe-quote ICU-escapes the < so it is not parsed as a markup tag; keep the literal text <dir> intact.

fileExplorer.dirSize.mismatchTooltipPref: Content and on-disk sizes differ significantly.

@fileExplorer.dirSize.mismatchTooltipPre.description: First line of the warning tooltip shown when a folder''s logical (content) and physical (on-disk) sizes differ a lot; the detailed size breakdown follows below it.

fileExplorer.dirSize.lowerBoundLine: At least this much: some subfolders weren''t scanned yet, so the real total may be larger.

@fileExplorer.dirSize.lowerBoundLine.description: Line in a folder''s size tooltip when the shown size is a lower bound (displayed with a ≥ prefix) because part of the subtree hasn''t been scanned. Explains the number is a floor: the real total is at least this much and may be larger (not necessarily larger — the unscanned parts could be empty).

fileExplorer.dirSize.unknownTooltip: Size unknown: this folder hasn''t been scanned yet.

@fileExplorer.dirSize.unknownTooltip.description: Hover tooltip on a folder whose recursive size is unknown (shown as an em dash —) because nothing in its subtree has been scanned. Distinct from a genuinely-empty folder, which shows 0 bytes.

fileExplorer.dirSize.staleLine: These sizes are from an earlier scan and may be out of date.

@fileExplorer.dirSize.staleLine.description: Line in a folder''s size tooltip when the size is exact but was computed at an older point (the drive hasn''t been re-scanned since reconnecting), so it''s accurate-as-of-then but possibly stale now.

@fileExplorer.selectionTooltip.selected.description: Label in the selection-summary size tooltip for the size of the selected items. Pairs with "Of total".

@fileExplorer.selectionTooltip.ofTotal.description: Label in the selection-summary size tooltip for the size of all items, against which the selection is compared. Pairs with "Selected".

@fileExplorer.selectionTooltip.onDiskHea.description: Section header in the selection-summary size tooltip introducing the physical on-disk sizes (as opposed to logical content sizes). Note the colon.

@fileExplorer.entry.brokenSymlink.description: Placeholder shown in the date column for a symlink whose target is missing. Shown in parentheses. A symlink is a file-system shortcut/alias.

@fileExplorer.entry.permissionDenied.description: Placeholder shown in the date column for a file Cmdr can''t read due to permissions. Shown in parentheses.

@fileExplorer.dateTooltip.created.description: Label in a file''s date tooltip for its creation time.

@fileExplorer.dateTooltip.lastOpened.description: Label in a file''s date tooltip for when it was last opened.

@fileExplorer.dateTooltip.lastMoved.description: Label in a file''s date tooltip for when it was last moved into its folder. The parenthetical "added" is the macOS Finder term for the same timestamp; keep both.

@fileExplorer.dateTooltip.lastModified.description: Label in a file''s date tooltip for when its contents were last changed.

@fileExplorer.imageIndex.file.indexed.description: Hover tooltip on a small badge over an image file''s icon in the file list. Means this image is fully indexed, so image search can find it by the text inside it.

@fileExplorer.imageIndex.file.pending.description: Hover tooltip on the badge over an image file''s icon. Means the image is queued for image-search indexing but hasn''t been processed yet.

fileExplorer.imageIndex.file.stale: Changed since indexing; will be re-indexed

@fileExplorer.imageIndex.file.stale.description: Hover tooltip on the badge over an image file''s icon. Means the file changed after it was indexed, so it''s scheduled to be indexed again.

@fileExplorer.imageIndex.file.failed.description: Hover tooltip on the badge over an image file''s icon. Means indexing this image didn''t work. Keep it gentle; do not use the words "error" or "failed" in the visible copy.

@fileExplorer.imageIndex.file.excluded.description: Hover tooltip on the badge over an image file''s icon. Means this image is outside what image search covers (excluded folder, out of scope, unsupported type, or too big), so it won''t be indexed.

@fileExplorer.imageIndex.folder.allIndex.description: Hover tooltip on a small badge over a folder''s icon in the file list. Means every image under this folder (and its subfolders) is indexed for image search. {totalText} is the preformatted image count; {total} is the raw number, used only to choose the singular/plural of ''image''.

placeholders.totalText: preformatted count of images under the folder, e.g. "50"
placeholders.total: the same count as a raw number, for singular/plural selection only

@fileExplorer.imageIndex.folder.someInde.description: Hover tooltip on a small badge over a folder''s icon in the file list. Shows how many images under this folder (and its subfolders) are indexed so far, e.g. "12 of 50 images indexed". {doneText}/{totalText} are the preformatted counts; {done}/{total} are the raw numbers, used only for singular/plural selection.

placeholders.doneText: preformatted count of images already indexed, e.g. "12"
placeholders.totalText: preformatted count of images eligible under the folder, e.g. "50"
placeholders.done: the indexed count as a raw number, for selection only
placeholders.total: the eligible count as a raw number, for singular/plural selection

fileExplorer.imageIndex.drive.ariaLabel: Image search status for this drive

@fileExplorer.imageIndex.drive.ariaLabel.description: Accessible label for the small colored dot next to a drive in the volume breadcrumb that shows the drive''s image-search indexing status.

fileExplorer.imageIndex.drive.off: Image search is off for this drive.

@fileExplorer.imageIndex.drive.off.description: Tooltip on the drive''s image-search dot when image search is turned off, so none of this drive''s images are indexed.

fileExplorer.imageIndex.drive.indexing: {doneText} of {totalText} {total, plural, one {image} other {images}} on this drive indexed; still working.

@fileExplorer.imageIndex.drive.indexing.description: Tooltip on the drive''s image-search dot while a pass is running or images are still pending. Shows drive-wide progress, e.g. "1,200 of 3,800 images on this drive indexed; still working." {doneText}/{totalText} are preformatted counts; {done}/{total} are raw numbers for singular/plural selection.

placeholders.doneText: preformatted count of images already indexed on the drive
placeholders.totalText: preformatted count of images to be indexed on the drive
placeholders.done: the indexed count as a raw number, for selection only
placeholders.total: the to-be-indexed count as a raw number, for singular/plural selection

fileExplorer.imageIndex.drive.done: All {totalText} {total, plural, one {image} other {images}} on this drive are indexed.

@fileExplorer.imageIndex.drive.done.description: Tooltip on the drive''s image-search dot when every in-scope image on the drive is indexed, e.g. "All 3,800 images on this drive are indexed." {totalText} is the preformatted count; {total} is the raw number for singular/plural selection.

placeholders.totalText: preformatted count of images indexed on the drive
placeholders.total: the same count as a raw number, for singular/plural selection

@fileExplorer.selectionInfo.nothingHere.description: Status-bar text shown below a pane when the current folder is empty.

@fileExplorer.selectionInfo.dir.description: Status-bar marker shown in place of a size for a folder whose recursive size is not computed. Short for "directory"; rendered in capitals.

@fileExplorer.selectionInfo.sizeNotReady.description: Accessibility label for the hourglass shown next to a folder size that is still being computed.

@fileExplorer.selectionInfo.sizeUpdating.description: Accessibility label for the hourglass shown next to a folder size that may still change while indexing.

@fileExplorer.selectionInfo.indexUpdatin.description: Hover tooltip on the status-bar hourglass while indexing, warning the size figure may change. Note the comma.

fileExplorer.selectionInfo.symlinkHint: This folder contains symlinks. Symlinked content is not counted in the total to avoid double counting.

@fileExplorer.selectionInfo.symlinkHint.description: Hover tooltip on the info icon next to a folder''s size, explaining that symlinked content is excluded from the total. A symlink is a file-system shortcut/alias.

@fileExplorer.selectionInfo.noSelectionF.description: File-count fragment of the no-selection status summary, e.g. "1,234 files". Combined into the sentence "No selection, 1,234 files and 12 dirs."

placeholders.countText: the number of files, already formatted for display
placeholders.count: the same number of files (drives the singular/plural form)

fileExplorer.selectionInfo.noSelectionDi: and {count, plural, one {{countText} dir} other {{countText} dirs}}

@fileExplorer.selectionInfo.noSelectionD.description: Folder-count fragment appended to the no-selection status summary when folders exist, e.g. " and 12 dirs". "dir" is short for directory/folder. Includes a leading space and the word "and".

placeholders.countText: the number of folders, already formatted for display
placeholders.count: the same number of folders (drives the singular/plural form)

@fileExplorer.selectionInfo.noSelection.description: Status-bar summary when nothing is selected, e.g. "No selection, 1,234 files and 12 dirs." {files} is the file-count fragment; {dirs} is the optional folder-count fragment (empty when there are no folders).

placeholders.dirs: the folder-count phrase including its leading " and ", or empty when there are no folders

@fileExplorer.tabBar.paneTabsAriaLabel.description: Accessibility label for one pane''s tab strip. {paneId} is the side, "left" or "right".

placeholders.paneId: which pane''s tabs these are: the value is left or right

@fileExplorer.tabBar.unreachableAriaLabe.description: Accessibility label and tooltip for a tab whose folder/volume can''t currently be reached.

@fileExplorer.tabBar.pinnedAriaLabel.description: Accessibility label and tooltip for a pinned tab (one kept open deliberately).

@fileExplorer.tabBar.closeTabTooltip.description: Hover tooltip for the small close button on a tab.

@fileExplorer.tabBar.newTabAriaLabel.description: Accessibility label and tooltip for the button that opens a new tab.

@fileExplorer.summary.fileNoun.description: The noun "file(s)" used in the status-bar selection summary, e.g. "3 of 10 files". A separate count (shown nearby in styled markup) precedes it; this key supplies only the singular/plural noun.

placeholders.count: the number the noun agrees with (drives singular vs plural)

@fileExplorer.summary.dirNoun.description: The noun "dir(s)" (short for folder) used in the status-bar selection summary, e.g. "2 of 5 dirs". A separate count precedes it in styled markup; this key supplies only the singular/plural noun.

placeholders.count: the number the noun agrees with (drives singular vs plural)

@fileExplorer.summary.of.description: The connector word between a selected size/count and the total in the status-bar selection summary, e.g. "5 MB of 20 MB" or "3 of 10 files". Surrounded by spaces in the rendered line.

@fileExplorer.summary.percentSelectedIn.description: Mid-sentence fragment of the status-bar selection summary stating the selected percentage of the total size, before the item counts. Example full line: "5 MB of 20 MB (25%) selected in 3 of 10 files".

placeholders.percent: the selected size as a percentage of the total (a whole number)

@fileExplorer.summary.and.description: The connector word joining the file part and the folder part of the status-bar selection summary, e.g. "3 of 10 files and 2 of 5 dirs". Rendered with surrounding spaces.

@fileExplorer.summary.selectedSuffix.description: Word appended in the dirs-only status-bar summary when no size is shown, e.g. "2 of 5 dirs selected." Only used in that no-size branch.

fileExplorer.renameConflict.title: A file with this name already exists

@fileExplorer.renameConflict.title.description: Title of the dialog shown when renaming a file to a name that already exists in the folder.

fileExplorer.renameConflict.description: "{name}" already exists in this folder. What would you like to do?

@fileExplorer.renameConflict.description.description: Body of the rename-conflict dialog, naming the existing file and asking how to proceed. {name} is the existing file''s name, shown in quotes.

placeholders.name: the name of the existing file that would be overwritten

@fileExplorer.renameConflict.yours.description: Header of the card showing the file the user is renaming (the new one) in the rename-conflict comparison. {name} is the file name; "(yours)" marks it as the user''s incoming file.

placeholders.name: the name of the file being renamed (the user''s file)

@fileExplorer.renameConflict.existing.description: Header of the card showing the file already in the folder in the rename-conflict comparison. {name} is the file name; "(existing)" marks it as the one already there.

placeholders.name: the name of the existing file already in the folder

@fileExplorer.renameConflict.size.description: Metadata label for a file''s size in the rename-conflict comparison cards.

@fileExplorer.renameConflict.modified.description: Metadata label for a file''s last-modified date in the rename-conflict comparison cards.

@fileExplorer.renameConflict.overwriteTr.description: Primary button in the rename-conflict dialog: replace the existing file, moving the old one to the trash (recoverable).

@fileExplorer.renameConflict.overwriteDe.description: Destructive button in the rename-conflict dialog: replace the existing file, permanently deleting the old one (not recoverable).

@fileExplorer.renameConflict.cancel.description: Button in the rename-conflict dialog that abandons the rename entirely.

@fileExplorer.renameConflict.continueRen.description: Button in the rename-conflict dialog that returns to editing the name so the user can choose a different one.

fileExplorer.extensionChange.title: Change file extension?

@fileExplorer.extensionChange.title.description: Title of the dialog confirming a change to a file''s extension during rename.

fileExplorer.extensionChange.description: Are you sure you want to change the extension from ".{oldExt}" to ".{newExt}"? Your file may open in a different app next time you open it.

@fileExplorer.extensionChange.descriptio.description: Body of the extension-change confirmation, warning that changing the extension may change which app opens the file. The leading dots are part of the displayed extensions.

placeholders.oldExt: the current file extension without the dot (e.g. txt)
placeholders.newExt: the new file extension without the dot (e.g. md)

@fileExplorer.extensionChange.alwaysAllo.description: Checkbox in the extension-change dialog to stop asking for confirmation on future extension changes.

@fileExplorer.extensionChange.keepOld.description: Button to keep the original extension and not change it. The leading dot is part of the displayed extension.

placeholders.oldExt: the current file extension without the dot (e.g. txt)

@fileExplorer.extensionChange.useNew.description: Button to apply the new extension. The leading dot is part of the displayed extension.

placeholders.newExt: the new file extension without the dot (e.g. md)

fileExplorer.quickLookHint.spaceSelects: In Cmdr, <space></space> selects the file under the cursor by default.

@fileExplorer.quickLookHint.spaceSelects.description: First line of the educational toast for users coming from Finder. The <space></space> tag wraps an inline keyboard-key chip showing the Space key. "Cmdr" is the app name; keep it. Reorder words naturally but keep the chip in the sentence.

placeholders.space: marks where the Space-key chip is shown inline; carries no translatable text

fileExplorer.quickLookHint.finderCompari: If you come from Finder, this might be unusual because Finder triggers a "Quick preview" action on <space></space>.

@fileExplorer.quickLookHint.finderCompar.description: Second line of the Finder-convert toast, contrasting Cmdr with Finder. The <space></space> tag wraps an inline Space-key chip. "Finder" is the macOS file manager and "Quick preview" its feature; keep both. Reorder words naturally but keep the chip.

placeholders.space: marks where the Space-key chip is shown inline; carries no translatable text

fileExplorer.quickLookHint.quickView: In Cmdr, you can trigger quick view via <key></key>. (<key></key> works in Finder, too, btw!)

@fileExplorer.quickLookHint.quickView.description: Third line of the Finder-convert toast, telling the user the Cmdr shortcut for quick view. Each <key></key> tag wraps an inline chip showing the same Quick Look shortcut (it appears twice). "Cmdr"/"Finder" are app names; "btw" is casual for "by the way". Keep both chips in the sentence.

placeholders.key: marks where the Quick Look shortcut chip is shown inline (appears twice, same shortcut); carries no translatable text

fileExplorer.quickLookHint.enterOpens: You can also use <enter></enter> to open files in the default app.

@fileExplorer.quickLookHint.enterOpens.description: Fourth line of the Finder-convert toast, noting the Enter key opens files. The <enter></enter> tag wraps an inline Enter-key chip. Reorder words naturally but keep the chip.

placeholders.enter: marks where the Enter-key chip is shown inline; carries no translatable text

fileExplorer.quickLookHint.configurable: All of this is configurable in <settingsLink>Settings > Keyboard shortcuts</settingsLink>.

@fileExplorer.quickLookHint.configurable.description: Last line of the Finder-convert toast, linking to the settings page. The <settingsLink>…</settingsLink> tag wraps the clickable link text, which names the Settings location; keep its label pointing at the Keyboard shortcuts area.

placeholders.settingsLink: the clickable link wrapping the text "Settings > Keyboard shortcuts"

@fileExplorer.quickLookHint.dontShowAgai.description: Button on the Finder-convert toast that permanently dismisses this hint (until re-enabled in Settings).

@fileExplorer.navigation.groupFavorites.description: Section heading in the volume switcher dropdown for the user''s pinned favorite folders.

@fileExplorer.navigation.groupVolumes.description: Section heading in the volume switcher dropdown for the main and attached disk volumes.

@fileExplorer.navigation.groupCloud.description: Section heading in the volume switcher dropdown for cloud-storage drives (for example iCloud, Dropbox).

@fileExplorer.navigation.groupMobile.description: Section heading in the volume switcher dropdown for connected phones and cameras (MTP devices).

@fileExplorer.navigation.groupNetwork.description: Section heading in the volume switcher dropdown for network locations (shared servers and mounted shares).

@fileExplorer.navigation.networkVolume.description: Name of the virtual Network entry in the volume switcher, which opens the network browser. Also shown as the current volume''s name in the breadcrumb when browsing the network.

@fileExplorer.navigation.networkVolumeDi.description: Name of the virtual Network entry in the volume switcher when networking is turned off in Settings; clicking it opens Settings instead of browsing. The "(disabled)" note tells the user why it won''t open.

@fileExplorer.navigation.searchResultsVo.description: The volume-slot label in the breadcrumb when viewing a saved search-results listing. A generic noun matching every other volume''s slot; the search-specific title appears separately in the path.

@fileExplorer.navigation.volumeFallback.description: Generic fallback shown as the current volume''s name in the breadcrumb when the real volume can''t be determined yet.

@fileExplorer.navigation.favoritesEmpty.description: Placeholder shown in the Favorites section of the volume switcher when the user has no favorites. Shown in parentheses as a disabled, non-clickable hint.

fileExplorer.navigation.favoriteTooltip:
{path}
Drag to reorder, or {reorder}. Right-click to rename or remove.

@fileExplorer.navigation.favoriteTooltip.description: Hover tooltip for a favorite row in the volume switcher. Leads with the folder path so a renamed favorite still reveals where it points, then explains how to reorder, rename, or remove it. The \n is a real line break. {reorder} is the keyboard reorder shortcut glyphs.

placeholders.path: the file-system path the favorite points to
placeholders.reorder: the keyboard reorder shortcut, e.g. ⌥↑ / ⌥↓ on macOS (do not translate; these are key names)

@fileExplorer.navigation.renameFavoriteA.description: Accessibility label for the inline text field that appears when renaming a favorite in the volume switcher.

fileExplorer.navigation.removeFavoriteFa: Couldn''t remove that favorite. Try again?

@fileExplorer.navigation.removeFavoriteF.description: Error toast when removing a favorite from the list did not work, inviting the user to retry.

fileExplorer.navigation.renameFavoriteFa: Couldn''t rename that favorite. Try again?

@fileExplorer.navigation.renameFavoriteF.description: Error toast when renaming a favorite did not work, inviting the user to retry.

@fileExplorer.navigation.reorderFavorite.description: Error toast when reordering favorites did not work, inviting the user to retry.

fileExplorer.navigation.locationUnreacha: Couldn''t reach that location''s drive. It might be disconnected.

@fileExplorer.navigation.locationUnreach.description: Toast shown when the app can't figure out which drive (volume) a place lives on, so it can't navigate there. Happens when going to a search result, a typed path, or a downloaded file whose drive is unplugged or no longer present. "Drive" means a storage volume like a hard disk or USB stick.

fileExplorer.navigation.connectionToolti: Connected directly for fast file operations

@fileExplorer.navigation.connectionToolt.description: Hover tooltip on the connection indicator of a network volume that is connected directly (the fast path).

fileExplorer.navigation.connectionToolti: Using system connection. Use the "Connect directly for faster access" menu item for faster access.

@fileExplorer.navigation.connectionToolt.description: Hover tooltip on the connection indicator of a network volume mounted via the OS (slower path), pointing the user at the menu item that upgrades to a direct connection. The quoted menu item label must match the menu item exactly.

@fileExplorer.navigation.connectDirectly.description: Menu item (in the breadcrumb popup and the dropdown submenu) that upgrades an OS-mounted network share to a faster direct connection.

@fileExplorer.navigation.connectingDirec.description: Persistent progress toast shown while upgrading a network share to a direct connection.

@fileExplorer.navigation.connectingWithS.description: Persistent progress toast shown while connecting to a network share using the password macOS already saved.

fileExplorer.navigation.useSavedPassword: Cmdr can reuse the password macOS already saved for "{displayName}". You''ll see a system prompt asking to allow Keychain access. That''s expected, so click Allow.

@fileExplorer.navigation.useSavedPasswor.description: Body of the confirmation dialog offering to reuse a network share''s saved macOS password. Warns that a macOS Keychain consent prompt (which Cmdr can''t customize) will follow. "Keychain" is the macOS credential store and "Allow" is the button on that system prompt — localize both to the terms your macOS uses (Apple localizes Keychain Access and the Allow button), per the Term-choice principles.

placeholders.displayName: the display name of the network share whose saved password would be reused

@fileExplorer.navigation.useSavedPasswor.description: Title of the confirmation dialog offering to reuse a network share''s saved macOS password.
Confirm button in the reuse-saved-password dialog: go ahead and use the password macOS saved.
Cancel button in the reuse-saved-password dialog: skip the saved password and type one instead.

@fileExplorer.navigation.readOnlyTooltip.description: Hover tooltip on the lock icon marking a read-only volume in the breadcrumb and volume switcher.

@fileExplorer.navigation.volumeOptionsTo.description: Hover tooltip on the breadcrumb options button (the chevron next to an OS-mounted network volume) that opens the connect-directly popup.

@fileExplorer.navigation.ejectVolumeAria.description: Accessibility label and tooltip for the eject button on a volume in the breadcrumb and switcher. {name} is the volume''s display name.

placeholders.name: the display name of the volume to eject

fileExplorer.navigation.ejectBusyTooltip: Can''t eject while operations are in progress on this device

@fileExplorer.navigation.ejectBusyToolti.description: Accessibility label and tooltip on a disabled eject button, explaining the volume can''t be ejected because a file operation is running on it.

@fileExplorer.navigation.usbSpeed.description: First line of the USB-speed tooltip on a volume, naming the negotiated USB standard and its peak throughput, e.g. "USB 3.2 Gen 2 (Max. 1000 MB/s)". "Max." is short for maximum and "MB/s" is megabytes per second; keep the unit.

placeholders.label: the negotiated USB standard name, e.g. USB 3.2 Gen 2 (do not translate; it is a spec name)
placeholders.mbps: the peak throughput in megabytes per second, already formatted

fileExplorer.navigation.usbSpeedNegotiat: Negotiated for this cable, port, and device

@fileExplorer.navigation.usbSpeedNegotia.description: Second line of the USB-speed tooltip on a volume, clarifying that the shown speed reflects the specific cable, port, and device combination. Shown below the speed line on its own row.

@fileExplorer.navigation.spaceRetryingAu.description: Hover tooltip on a volume''s disk-space bar while the app automatically retries fetching its free space. Ends with an ellipsis character (…).

@fileExplorer.navigation.spaceRetrying.description: Hover tooltip on a volume''s disk-space bar while a manual retry of fetching its free space is in progress. Ends with an ellipsis character (…).

@fileExplorer.navigation.spaceRetryingTe.description: Short inline text shown in place of a volume''s disk-space figure while a retry is in progress. Tight space; keep it short.

@fileExplorer.navigation.spaceStillUnava.description: Hover tooltip on a volume''s disk-space bar after a retry to fetch free space also failed, inviting another click to retry.

fileExplorer.navigation.spaceFetchFailed: Couldn''t fetch disk space. Click to retry

@fileExplorer.navigation.spaceFetchFaile.description: Hover tooltip on a volume''s disk-space bar when fetching free space failed (first time), inviting a click to retry.

@fileExplorer.navigation.spaceUnavailabl.description: Short inline text shown in place of a volume''s disk-space figure when its free space couldn''t be fetched. Tight space; keep it short.

fileExplorer.navigation.spaceMtpHint: On a phone, this also counts apps and system files. Over USB, Cmdr can reach only your shared files, so the folders here add up to less than the used space.

@fileExplorer.navigation.spaceMtpHint.description: Hover tooltip on a connected phone''s disk-space readout (the free/total figure and its usage bar), shown only for phones reached over USB (MTP). Explains why the visible folders add up to far less than the space reported as used: a phone''s apps and system data count toward usage but can''t be browsed over USB. "USB" is the cable connection; "Cmdr" is the app name (do not translate).

@fileExplorer.navigation.volumesStillUnr.description: Warning row at the bottom of the volume switcher after a retry to load the volume list also failed, suggesting the user try again later.

@fileExplorer.navigation.volumesMayBeMis.description: Warning row at the bottom of the volume switcher when loading the volume list timed out, so the list may be incomplete.

@fileExplorer.navigation.refreshVolumeLi.description: Hover tooltip on the retry button in the volume switcher''s missing-volumes warning row, which reloads the volume list.

@fileExplorer.navigation.driveIndex.aria.description: Accessibility label, read aloud by screen readers, for the small colored dot next to a drive in the volume switcher that shows whether the drive is indexed and up to date. The color and the live detail are in its tooltip.

fileExplorer.navigation.driveIndex.toolt: Indexing is off for this drive. Turn it on to see folder sizes and search here.

@fileExplorer.navigation.driveIndex.tool.description: Hover tooltip on the gray drive-index dot, shown when a drive isn''t indexed. Invites the user to turn indexing on for folder sizes and search.

fileExplorer.navigation.driveIndex.toolt: Indexed and up to date. Last indexed {date}, took {duration}.

@fileExplorer.navigation.driveIndex.tool.description: Hover tooltip on the green drive-index dot when the drive''s index is current. Names when it was last indexed and how long that scan took.

placeholders.date: ISO date of the last completed scan, already formatted (e.g. 2026-06-19)
placeholders.duration: how long the last scan took, already formatted (e.g. 2 min, 14 s)

@fileExplorer.navigation.driveIndex.tool.description: Hover tooltip on the green drive-index dot when the index is current but the last scan''s date and duration aren''t known (no completed scan recorded). Shorter variant of the fresh tooltip.

fileExplorer.navigation.driveIndex.toolt: This drive may have changed while it was disconnected. Sizes and search might be a bit off. Rescan to refresh.

@fileExplorer.navigation.driveIndex.tool.description: Hover tooltip on the yellow drive-index dot, shown when the drive''s index may be out of date because the drive was disconnected. Active voice; never use the words ''error'' or ''failed''.

fileExplorer.navigation.driveIndex.toolt: Indexing stopped because of a problem saving this drive''s index. Rescan to rebuild it.

@fileExplorer.navigation.driveIndex.tool.description: Hover tooltip on the RED drive-index dot, shown when the drive''s index database hit a fatal storage problem (a disk I/O failure, a full or read-only disk, corruption) and indexing stopped for this drive. The index is a rebuildable cache, so the fix is to rescan. Keep it calm and actionable; active voice; never use the words ''error'' or ''failed''.

fileExplorer.navigation.driveIndex.toolt: macOS lost track of file system changes {count, plural, one {once} other {{countText} times}} in the last {hours, plural, one {hour} other {{hoursText} hours}}, so a few folder sizes might be slightly off. It''s usually caches full of small files, and Cmdr''s next full check in {remaining, plural, one {an hour} other {{remainingText} hours}} will fix it.

@fileExplorer.navigation.driveIndex.tool.description: Extra paragraph appended to the drive-index dot''s hover tooltip when macOS reported it had lost track of file system changes on this drive since the last full check, and Cmdr deliberately waited instead of rescanning right away. Reassuring, not alarming: the dot stays green because waiting is the intended behavior, and the next scheduled full check will bring folder sizes back in line. Keep the plural branches for all three numbers. Never use the words ''error'' or ''failed''.

placeholders.count: how many times macOS lost track since the last full check, as a raw number, used only to pick the plural branch
placeholders.countText: the same count, already formatted for the reader (e.g. 12)
placeholders.hours: whole hours since the drive''s last completed full check, as a raw number, used only to pick the plural branch (never below 1)
placeholders.hoursText: the same hour count, already formatted for the reader (e.g. 18)
placeholders.remaining: whole hours until the next scheduled full check, as a raw number, used only to pick the plural branch (never below 1)
placeholders.remainingText: the same remaining-hour count, already formatted for the reader (e.g. 6)

fileExplorer.navigation.driveIndex.toolt: macOS lost track of file system changes {count, plural, one {once} other {{countText} times}} in the last {hours, plural, one {hour} other {{hoursText} hours}}, so a few folder sizes might be slightly off. It''s usually caches full of small files, so it''s no big deal.

@fileExplorer.navigation.driveIndex.tool.description: The variant of the tooltip paragraph above for a drive with no scheduled full check to promise (an external drive, or one whose check is already due). Same first sentence; the closing reassurance replaces the ''next full check in N hours'' promise, so don''t reintroduce a time here. Never use the words ''error'' or ''failed''.

placeholders.count: how many times macOS lost track since the last full check, as a raw number, used only to pick the plural branch
placeholders.countText: the same count, already formatted for the reader (e.g. 12)
placeholders.hours: whole hours since the drive''s last completed full check, as a raw number, used only to pick the plural branch (never below 1)
placeholders.hoursText: the same hour count, already formatted for the reader (e.g. 18)

fileExplorer.navigation.driveIndex.menuE: Turn on indexing for this drive

@fileExplorer.navigation.driveIndex.menu.description: Menu item to start indexing the drive (shown when indexing is off). Sentence case.
Menu item to rebuild the drive''s index from scratch (shown when the drive is already indexed). Sentence case.

fileExplorer.navigation.driveIndex.menuD: Turn off indexing for this drive

@fileExplorer.navigation.driveIndex.menu.description: Menu item to stop indexing the drive and drop its live index (shown when the drive is indexed). Sentence case.
Menu item to cancel an in-progress scan (shown while the drive is being indexed). Sentence case.

Menu item that deletes the drive''s index entirely (removes its database from disk), so a fresh full scan starts next time the user turns indexing on. The recovery action when an index is stuck. More destructive than ''Turn off indexing'', which keeps the index to resume. Sentence case.

@fileExplorer.navigation.driveIndex.foot.description: Non-interactive footer at the bottom of the drive-index menu, naming when the drive was last indexed and how long that scan took. The middle dot (·) is a separator; keep it.

placeholders.date: ISO date of the last completed scan, already formatted (e.g. 2026-06-19)
placeholders.duration: how long the last scan took, already formatted (e.g. 2 min, 14 s)

@fileExplorer.navigation.driveIndex.dura.description: A scan duration of a minute or more, e.g. ''2 min, 14 s''. ''min'' and ''s'' abbreviate minutes and seconds.
A scan duration under a minute, e.g. ''14 s''. ''s'' abbreviates seconds.

fileExplorer.navigation.driveIndex.refus: Cmdr couldn''t connect to {name} directly, which it needs to index this share. Check the share is reachable and try again.

@fileExplorer.navigation.driveIndex.refu.description: Toast shown when turning on indexing for an SMB share doesn''t go through because the direct connection couldn''t be established. Active voice; avoid the words ''error'' and ''failed''.

fileExplorer.navigation.driveIndex.refus: {name} is disconnected. Reconnect it, then turn on indexing.

@fileExplorer.navigation.driveIndex.refu.description: Toast shown when turning on indexing for an SMB share is refused because the share is currently disconnected.

fileExplorer.navigation.driveIndex.refus: Cmdr can''t index {name} right now. Reconnect the drive and try again.

@fileExplorer.navigation.driveIndex.refu.description: Fallback toast when turning on indexing for a drive doesn''t go through because the per-drive command threw an unexpected exception (not one of the typed refusal reasons).

fileExplorer.navigation.driveIndex.refus: Cmdr hit a snag turning on indexing for {name}. This shouldn''t happen. Restarting Cmdr usually clears it.

@fileExplorer.navigation.driveIndex.refu.description: Toast shown when turning on indexing for a drive is refused for a reason that signals an internal problem rather than something the user can fix by reconnecting (the drive isn''t registered, or it''s classified as a non-SMB volume when it shouldn''t be). Reassuring and conversational; avoid the words ''error'' and ''failed''.
